title: tragacantha umbellata
intro: Tragacantha umbellata, also known as the milk thistle, is a biennial or perennial plant that is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its purple or white flowers and spiny leaves. The medicinal properties of Tragacantha umbellata lie in its seeds, which contain a high concentration of silymarin, a flavonoid complex that has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Tragacantha umbellata include protecting the liver from damage and promoting its regeneration, as well as reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. In herbal preparations, Tragacantha umbellata is commonly used in capsule or tea form to support liver health and overall well-being.
